mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by >0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand expansion, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero average 60-day return in more than 70% of occurrences during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 60% of months where INDPRO increases by more than 0.5% during early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 240.0, inflation expectations accelerate and force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of months where CPIAUCSL crosses above 240.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which elevates wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of observed months over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real gross domestic product values indicate stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate profit growth, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.05 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the industrial production index signal a contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain bottlenecks, which reduces earnings forecasts and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances following two consecutive monthly INDPRO declines.
